title: n8n Multi-Agent State Management: Keeping Memory Across Steps
description: Learn how to share state and memory between multiple AI agent nodes in an n8n workflow so each step has context from previous actions.

resources.title: n8n AI Agent Node Documentation
resources.description: Official docs on the n8n AI Agent node, memory configuration, and tool connections

resources.title: n8n Memory Nodes
resources.description: Overview of buffer memory, window memory, and other state management nodes in n8n

resources.title: Build a stateful multi-agent workflow
resources.description: Create an n8n workflow where Agent A researches a topic and Agent B writes a report using Agent A's findings
resources.command: # In n8n UI: Add AI Agent node → Connect Window Buffer Memory → Add second AI Agent node → Pass $json.output from first agent

resources.title: n8n Multi-Agent State Management Tutorial
resources.search_keyword: n8n multi-agent memory state management workflow 2026

quiz.question: What is the primary purpose of sharing state and memory between AI agent nodes in an n8n workflow?
options: To ensure each agent can operate independently without context
To allow each step to have context from previous actions
To increase the processing speed of each agent
To eliminate the need for agent connections
quiz.explanation: Sharing state and memory allows each step to build on the context of previous actions, enhancing the workflow's coherence.

quiz.question: Which memory configuration type is NOT mentioned in the n8n documentation?
quiz.explanation: Stack memory is not mentioned in the context of n8n's memory configuration types, while buffer and window memory are.

quiz.question: In an n8n workflow, if Agent A researches a topic, what is Agent B's role?
options: To conduct a similar research task
To write a report using Agent A's findings
To analyze the data collected by Agent A
To present the findings to a human user
quiz.explanation: Agent B's role is to utilize the findings from Agent A's research to write a report, demonstrating the sharing of context.

practicePrompts.label: Research and Report
practicePrompts.instruction: Create a workflow for two agents.
practicePrompts.prompt: Agent A, research the benefits of renewable energy. Agent B, summarize Agent A's findings in a report.
practicePrompts.followUp: Try adding a third agent to edit the report based on feedback.

practicePrompts.label: Memory Configuration Test
practicePrompts.prompt: Set up an n8n workflow with buffer memory for Agent A to store data from previous steps. How does it affect Agent B's output?
practicePrompts.followUp: Switch to window memory and compare the results.

practicePrompts.label: State Sharing Challenge
practicePrompts.prompt: Create an n8n workflow where Agent A collects user preferences and Agent B uses this data to recommend products. How does state sharing improve the interaction?
practicePrompts.followUp: Add conditions based on user preferences to enhance recommendations.
